data.title: Roche licenses cancer candidate from China’s Hansoh Pharma
data.description:
Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) has signed an agreement with China's Hansoh Pharma to obtain exclusive licensing rights for a cancer candidate called HS-20110, the Hong Kong-listed firm announced.

The antibody drug conjugate, which has demonstrated early clinical activity against colorectal cancer and other solid tumors, is currently undergoing a global Phase 1 study in the U.S. and China, according to a statement from Hansoh.

As part of the agreement, Roche (OTCQX:RHHBF) will receive licensing rights to develop and commercialize HS-20110 globally, excluding Mainland China, Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan.

In exchange, Hansoh will receive $80M upfront, in addition to development, regulatory approval, and commercialization milestone payments, as well as royalties on potential drug sales.

“Partnering with Roche enables HS-20110 to move faster toward potential approval and patient access, furthering Hansoh’s commitment to bringing potentially transformative treatments to people with cancer around the world,” said Eliza Sun, an executive director of the Hansoh board.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Ally Financial beats Q3 estimates, written premiums of $385 million, flat Y/Y
Fabrinet founder Mitchell to retire
data.description:
• Fabrinet (NYSE:FN) said on Friday that founder and Chairman Tom Mitchell will step down and retire after 25 years of leading the electronic manufacturing services company.
  • Mitchell founded Fabrinet in 2000 and served as the company's CEO and Chairman until 2017. At that point, Seamus Grady was appointed CEO. Mitchell returned to his role as Chairman in 2018. 
  • In addition to starting Fabrinet, Mitchell also previously co-founded Seagate Technologies (STX).
  • “Seeing Fabrinet grow from a start-up to a multi-billion-dollar organization has been the highlight of my professional career,” said Mitchell in a statement. “I have taken great pride in seeing the core values we established at inception – exceptional customer service, advanced manufacturing innovation, and delivering a compelling value proposition – continue to guide the business. With Seamus Grady at the helm, I am confident that the enduring power of these values will continue to strengthen Fabrinet’s position as a market leader.”
  • Fabrinet shares were little changed in premarket trading.

data.title: Rani Therapeutics secures $60.3M in private placement to fund pipeline through 2028
data.description:
• Rani Therapeutics (NASDAQ:RANI) announced a $60.3M private placement of Class A common stock (or pre-funded warrants), and accompanying warrants.
  • Executive Chairman Mir Imran participated in the oversubscribed offering, which is expected to close on or about October 21, 2025.
  • The offering was priced "at-the-market" under Nasdaq rules; warrant exercise is subject to stockholder approval.
  • Combined with a $10M upfront payment and an anticipated $18M milestone payment from its partnership with Chugai Pharmaceuticals, the funds are expected to extend operations into 2028.
  • Proceeds will support further development of the RaniPill oral biologics delivery platform and the company’s therapeutic pipeline.

data.title: American Express in charts: Q3 network volumes +8.7%, provisions for credit losses -5% Y/Y
European defense stocks sink as Trump sets another meeting with Putin
data.description:
European defense stocks tumbled Friday after President Trump and Russian counterpart Putin agreed to another summit meeting to discuss the war in Ukraine, possibly within the next two weeks in Budapest.

The sector had hit record highs during the week as the European Union proposed a reform of its military planning and procurement, aimed at making the bloc ready for combat by the end of the decade, as relations with Russia continue to deteriorate.

In European trading: Germany's Rheinmetall (OTCPK:RNMBF) (OTCPK:RNMBY) -6.7%, Germany's Hensoldt (OTCPK:HAGHY) (OTCPK:HNSDF) -7.6%, Germany's Renk (OTCPK:RNKGF) -5.5%, Italy's Leonardo (OTCPK:FINMF) (OTCPK:FINMY) -4.6%, Sweden's Saab (OTCPK:SAABY) -3.5%, Norway's Kongsberg Gruppen (OTCPK:NSKFF) (OTCPK:KBGGY) -5.3%, U.K.'s BAE Systems (OTCPK:BAESF) (OTCPK:BAESY) -3%, U.K.'s Babcock (OTCPK:BCKIF) -3.5%, Spain's Indra Sistemas (OTCPK:ISMAF) (OTCPK:ISMAY) -2.7%, France's Thales (OTCPK:THLEF) (OTCPK:THLLY) -2.5%, France's Dassault (OTCPK:DUAVF) -0.8%.

Friday's moves could reverse if there is no concrete result from the summit, as it was the case after the August meeting in Alaska.

data.title: Webster Financial GAAP EPS of $1.54 beats by $0.01, revenue of $732.6M beats by $3.96M
data.description:
• Webster Financial press release (NYSE:WBS): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.54 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $732.6M (+13.1% Y/Y) beats by $3.96M.
  • Period end loans and leases balance of $55.1 billion, up $1.4 billion, or 2.6 percent from prior quarter.
  • Period end deposits balance of $68.2 billion, up $1.9 billion, or 2.8 percent, from prior quarter.
  • Provision for credit losses of $44.0 million.
  • Return on average assets of 1.27 percent.
  • Return on average tangible common equity of 17.64 percent1.
  • Net interest margin of 3.40 percent, down 4 basis points from prior quarter.
  • Common equity tier 1 ratio of 11.40 percent.
  • Efficiency ratio of 45.79 percent.
  • Tangible common equity ratio of 7.50 percent.

data.title: Prospect Ridge announces $3M flow-through private placement
data.description:
• Prospect Ridge Resources (OTCQB:PRRSF) has announced a non-brokered private placement of up to $3M via issuance of up to 25M critical mineral flow-through units priced at $0.12 per unit.
  • Each unit includes one flow-through common share and one-half of a warrant; full warrants are exercisable at $0.18 for two years.
  • Proceeds will fund eligible Canadian exploration expenses, primarily a 2,000-meter drill program at the Camelot project near Horsefly, B.C.

data.title: Ally Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $1.15 beats by $0.14, revenue of $2.16B beats by $50M
data.description:
• Ally Financial press release (NYSE:ALLY): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.15 beats by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $2.16B (+3.3% Y/Y) beats by $50M.
  • NIM ex. OID1 of 3.55% is up 10 bps quarter over quarter
  • Common equity tier 1 ratio of 10.1% increased ~20 bps quarter over quarter | Fully phased-in AOCI CET1 of 8.0% up ~90 bps since year-end 2024

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: American Express tops Q3 estimates; provisions for credit losses were $1.3B
